enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a brief late-night vlog update for November 1, 2016, covering his day at work and progress on a Google Earth art project using skewers. He also shares an interesting discovery about a forgotten WWII military base in Colorado and discusses his goal of finishing his Google Earth Boulder project by year's end.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: wants to finish the skewers project this week as a side goal, with Boulder County as the main year-end goal
believes making videos makes him more well-rounded and may motivate him to do more surface-level website work like AdWords and fixing links
finds it fascinating and compares it to a Roman camp — thinks it's really cool that the foundations are still visible on Google Earth

transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder V-log for the first of November. It's actually the second now. It's 12.53 a.m. I'm glad that I remembered to do the video before I got too tired.

And, um, so today what happened was I, um, got up and I went to work and then, uh, we did one place and then I came home and, um, I remember my mom wanted me to help her out, like, cleaning the house and stuff.
But I totally spaced calling her and see if she needed any help. So I just worked all day long on my, uh, Google Earth Colorado Ski Resort project.
Um, I predicted I'd be done this week, so I will, I guess Friday, so it's Tuesday. I have three more days. Um, today I, uh, see.
When I made that prediction that I'd be done, um, then, um, I didn't consider, uh, that, um, I didn't actually look at how many ski resorts I had to do.
I, because I remembered when I built those, started the project like a year ago, whatever, um, there were some that I didn't, I didn't actually get to the end of the line.
And so I actually did get to the end of the line today and there were a few more, more than I thought of actual ski resorts.
Like, that you could see the roads cut out of the trees. There's a whole bunch of them.
Um, and then I found out about this cool place that I didn't forgot about or didn't know about adequately is, uh, this, um, army base that was in Colorado in World War II.
They had 15,000 soldiers there and now if you look at it, nothing exists.
Um, but if you look at it on Google Earth, you can see all of the foundations to all the houses and you can trace exactly how big it is.
So it's like a prefab, like, soldier camp. That's pretty fucking cool.
Like a modern day version of, like, a Roman camp.
Uh, sort of. So, that would be like three armies, 15,000 people, three legions.
So, uh, um, that's what Varys lost in the Arcanian Forest.
So, um, yeah, and so I finished those ski resorts and then, uh, it's gonna be cool to look at.
And then, uh, I'm organizing it on the Dreamweaver Narrow page and the webpage.
And then, um, yeah, maybe tomorrow I can finish the, uh, defunct ski resorts and then finish the whole project this week.
Um, and then I'll get going on, uh, Boulder. I really, really, really, I'm still, like, in full blast.
Um, now that I'm making videos, I'm more well-rounded and so maybe it'll increase the chances that I will, um, do more surface level stuff on my website.
Like, add words and fix links, but, um, and add ads.
But, uh, I think after I've done it with the, uh, ski resort, I'll have to go ahead and hammer away at the, um, Google Earth Boulder.
Because, which will lead into Boulder County probably because I kinda wanted to finish that.
Like, that was my goal to finish by the end of the year.
Like, this little ski resort, uh, side, um, Segway.
My goal was to finish this week, but it's Google Earth Boulder that I wanted to finish this year.
So, but whatever.
Uh, if not, it'll be this winter because I do, there are some other things that I want to do in Google Earth, like the 400 mountains from Boulder.
So, uh, I was listening to, uh, Arcadius and Honorius on Decline and Fall.
And some more of those videos on history scientists use good.
Um, starting to figure, feel like I'm figuring it out.
Um, it's awesome to listen to all day long.
Uh, wow, I'm really tired though.
How long is the video?
Okay, I've been talking long enough.
Peace out.
